name: Assessment Merger & Trust Scoring
description: Merges multiple reviewer Assessments into a single ReviewReport using Arbiter trust-weighted scoring. Conflict resolution: security BLOCK overrides style PASS with explanation; configurable priority ordering. Per-finding confidence scores derived from reviewer trust weight × finding confidence. Deduplicates overlapping findings across reviewers. Produces final ReviewDecision (block/warn/pass) based on configurable thresholds. Emits assessment.merged Chronicler event via fire-and-forget callback.

types.description: Severity level of a review finding, ordered from most to least critical.

types.name: ReviewDecision
types.description: Final decision on a pull request review: block merging, warn, or pass.

types.name: MergeContext

fields.name: default_trust_weight
fields.description: Fallback trust weight when no explicit TrustScore is provided for a reviewer+stage.

fields.name: block_threshold
fields.description: Aggregate score at or above which the final decision is BLOCK.

fields.description: Aggregate score at or above which the final decision is WARN (below block_threshold).

fields.description: Ordered list of stages from highest to lowest priority for conflict resolution.

validators.error_message: stage_priority must contain at least one stage

fields.name: security_block_overrides
fields.description: If true, any security-stage BLOCK unconditionally forces the final decision to BLOCK.

types.description: Internal frozen Pydantic model holding extracted configuration values relevant to the merge algorithm. Constructed from ExemplarConfig at Assessor instantiation.

types.description: A hash-chain tamper-proof seal over serialized content, verifiable independently.

functions.name: merge_assessments
functions.description: Merges multiple reviewer Assessments into a single ReviewReport using trust-weighted scoring, deduplication, conflict resolution, and categorical overrides. This is the sole public method of AssessorProtocol. Executes a 4-phase algorithm: (1) resolve trust scores for each assessment, (2) deduplicate findings by composite key, (3) compute decision from weighted scores and categorical overrides, (4) build deterministic ReviewReport and emit assessment.merged Chronicler event.

inputs.name: assessments
inputs.description: List of Assessment objects from individual reviewers to merge. May be empty (produces a pass decision with no findings).

inputs.name: trust_scores
inputs.description: List of TrustScore objects mapping reviewer_id+stage to trust weights. Missing entries fall back to config default_trust_weight.

inputs.name: review_request_id
inputs.description: Unique identifier for the review request being assessed. Used for correlation across all outputs and the Chronicler event.

validators.error_message: review_request_id must be between 1 and 256 characters

error_cases.name: invalid_review_request_id
error_cases.condition: review_request_id is empty or exceeds 256 characters

error_data.detail: review_request_id must be a non-empty string of at most 256 characters

error_cases.name: assessment_request_id_mismatch
error_cases.condition: Any Assessment.review_request_id does not match the provided review_request_id

error_data.detail: All assessments must have review_request_id matching the provided review_request_id

error_cases.name: invalid_trust_weight
error_cases.condition: Any TrustScore.weight is outside the range [0.0, 1.0]

error_data.detail: All trust score weights must be in the range [0.0, 1.0]

error_cases.name: invalid_finding_confidence
error_cases.condition: Any Finding.confidence is outside the range [0.0, 1.0]

error_data.detail: All finding confidence scores must be in the range [0.0, 1.0]

error_cases.name: chronicler_emission_failure
error_cases.condition: ChroniclerEmitter raises an exception during event emission. This is fire-and-forget: the error is logged but does not propagate.

error_data.behavior: Logged and suppressed; ReviewReport is still returned successfully

error_cases.name: duplicate_assessment_reviewer_stage
error_cases.condition: Two or more assessments share the same (reviewer_id, stage) pair, indicating a duplicate submission

error_data.detail: Each (reviewer_id, stage) pair must be unique across assessments

preconditions: review_request_id is a non-empty string of at most 256 characters
All Assessment objects in assessments have review_request_id matching the provided review_request_id
Each Assessment contains a valid reviewer_id and stage
Each Finding within each Assessment has valid severity, confidence in [0.0, 1.0], and positive line_number
Assessor was constructed with valid MergeContext (block_threshold >= warn_threshold >= 0)
postconditions: Returned ReviewReport.findings is sorted by (file_path ASC, line_number ASC, severity DESC, id ASC)
No duplicate findings exist in output: each composite key (hunk_id, file_path, line_number, rule_id) appears at most once
If any security-stage assessment has decision BLOCK and security_block_overrides is True, final decision is BLOCK
If total_score >= block_threshold, decision is BLOCK (unless overridden to a stricter categorical BLOCK)
If total_score >= warn_threshold and total_score < block_threshold, decision is WARN
If total_score < warn_threshold and no categorical override applies, decision is PASS
Each finding's confidence == min(1.0, max(contributing trust weights) × original confidence)
All conflict_notes are sorted by stage priority (highest priority stage first)
Returned ReviewReport.timestamp is the value returned by the injected clock
An assessment.merged ChroniclerEvent was emitted (fire-and-forget, failure does not affect return value)
If assessments is empty, decision is PASS, findings is empty, total_score is 0.0, computed_confidence is 1.0

structured_side_effects.description: Logs merge phases, trust resolution fallbacks, deduplication statistics, and final decision at DEBUG/INFO level.
Fire-and-forget emission of assessment.merged ChroniclerEvent to Chronicler service. Failure is caught, logged, and suppressed.

performance_budget.complexity: O(n log n) where n = total findings across all assessments

invariants: All data models are frozen (Pydantic frozen=True); no mutation after construction
The Assessor is constructed with a valid MergeContext where block_threshold >= warn_threshold >= 0.0
The injected clock callable always returns a valid ISO 8601 timestamp string
ChroniclerEmitter failures are always caught and logged; they never propagate to callers
PACT_COMPONENT constant is always 'assessor' and is embedded in every ReviewReport and ChroniclerEvent
DEFAULT_TRUST_WEIGHT is 0.5 and is used as the fallback when no explicit TrustScore matches a (reviewer_id, stage) pair
SEVERITY_SCORE maps info=1.0, warning=3.0, error=7.0, critical=15.0
STAGE_PRIORITY ordering is [security, correctness, architecture, style] from highest to lowest priority
Finding sort order is deterministic: (file_path ASC, line_number ASC, severity DESC, id ASC)
Conflict notes sort order follows stage priority: higher priority stages appear first
Deduplication uses composite key (hunk_id, file_path, line_number, rule_id); within a duplicate group the highest severity finding is kept
For deduplicated findings, confidence is boosted to min(1.0, max_contributing_trust_weight × original_confidence)
Empty assessments input produces a PASS decision with no findings, total_score=0.0, and computed_confidence=1.0
A security-stage BLOCK categorical override (when enabled) takes absolute precedence over score-based thresholds
Each (reviewer_id, stage) pair must be unique across the input assessments list

data_access.rationale: Reads reviewer Assessment objects containing code review findings (file paths, line numbers, rule violations, severity levels) and TrustScore objects mapping reviewer identities to trust weights. All data is INTERNAL classification: it contains source code location metadata and internal review decisions but no PII or credentials. Writes a merged ReviewReport to the caller and emits an assessment.merged ChroniclerEvent containing the review decision, aggregate score, and finding count for operational observability.

side_effects.rationale: Emits an assessment.merged event to the Chronicler service for governance audit trail and operational observability. Contains the merge outcome summary but no raw source code content.

Structured logging at DEBUG/INFO level for merge diagnostics: trust resolution fallbacks, deduplication statistics, conflict resolutions, and final decision. Includes reviewer identifiers and review metadata for troubleshooting.

authority.rationale: The assessor is the authoritative component for computing the final merged ReviewDecision from multiple reviewer assessments. It owns the trust-weighted scoring algorithm, the finding deduplication logic (composite key resolution and confidence boosting), and the conflict resolution rules (categorical overrides and threshold-based decisions). No other component in the Exemplar system may independently compute a final review decision or deduplicate findings across reviewers.
